content:
# Matthew Chapter 22 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_022_page_001.jpg, MAT_022_page_002.jpg

1. And answering, the Yeshua again spoke to them in parables, saying,

2. "The kingdom of the heavens has been likened to a man, a king, who made a wedding-feast for the son of him.

3. And he sent the slaves of him to call the [ones] having been invited to the wedding-feast, and they did not wish to come.

4. Again he sent other slaves, saying, 'Tell the [ones] having been invited: Behold, the dinner of me I have prepared, the bulls of me and the fattened [animals] have been slaughtered and all things [are] ready; come to the wedding-feast.'

5. But they, having been unconcerned, went away, one to the own field, and one to the business of him.

6. And the rest, having seized the slaves of him, mistreated and killed [them].

7. And the king was angered, and having sent the armies of him, he destroyed the murderers those and the city of them burned.

8. Then he says to the slaves of him, 'The wedding-feast indeed is ready, but the [ones] having been invited were not worthy.

9. Go therefore to the crossroads of the roads, and as many as you find, invite to the wedding-feast.'

10. And having gone out, the slaves those into the roads gathered all whom they found, both evil and good; and the wedding-hall was filled with [those] reclining.

11. And having entered, the king to see the [ones] reclining saw there a man not having been clothed [in] a wedding-garment.

12. And he says to him, 'Friend, how did you enter here not having a wedding-garment?' And he was silenced.

13. Then the king said to the servants, 'Having bound the feet and hands of him, cast him out into the outer darkness; there will be the weeping and the gnashing of the teeth.'

14. For many are called, but few [are] chosen."

15. Then having gone, the Pharisaioi took counsel how they might trap him in [a] word.

16. And they send to him the disciples of them with the Herodianoi, saying, "Teacher, we know that you are true and the way of the God in truth you teach, and it does not matter to you concerning anyone, for you do not look at [the] face of men.

17. Tell us therefore, what do you think? Is it lawful to give poll-tax to Kaisar or not?"

18. And having known the evil of them, the Yeshua said, "Why do you test me, hypocrites?

19. Show me the coin of the poll-tax." And they brought to him a denarius.

20. And he says to them, "Whose [is] this image and the inscription?"

21. They say to him, "Kaisar's." Then he says to them, "Give therefore the [things] of Kaisar to Kaisar, and the [things] of the God to the God."

22. And having heard, they marveled, and having left him, they went away.

23. In that day Saddoukaioi came to him, saying [there is] no resurrection, and they asked him,

24. saying, "Teacher, Moses said, 'If anyone dies not having children, the brother of him will marry the wife of him and will raise up seed for the brother of him.'

25. And there were with us seven brothers. And the first, having married, died, and not having seed left the wife of him to the brother of him.

26. Likewise also the second and the third, until the seven.

27. And last of all the woman died.

28. In the resurrection therefore, of which of the seven will she be wife? For all had her."

29. And answering, the Yeshua said to them, "You are deceived, not knowing the Scriptures nor the power of the God.

30. For in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given-in-marriage, but are as angels in the heaven.

31. But concerning the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what was spoken to you by the God, saying,

32. 'I am the God of Abraham and the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob'? He is not the God of [the] dead but of [the] living."

33. And having heard, the crowds were astonished at the teaching of him.

34. And the Pharisaioi, having heard that he silenced the Saddoukaioi, were gathered together.

35. And one of them, a lawyer, asked, testing him,

36. "Teacher, which commandment [is] great in the law?"

37. And he said to him, "'You shall love [the] Lord the God of you with the whole heart of you and with the whole soul of you and with the whole mind of you.'

38. This is the great and first commandment.

39. And [the] second [is] like it: 'You shall love the neighbor of you as yourself.'

40. On these two commandments the whole law hangs, and the prophets."

41. And the Pharisaioi having been gathered, the Yeshua asked them,

42. saying, "What do you think concerning the Christos? Whose son is he?" They say to him, "Of David."

43. He says to them, "How then does David in [the] Spirit call him Lord, saying,

44. 'The Lord said to the Lord of me, "Sit at the right of me until I place the enemies of you beneath the feet of you"'?

45. If therefore David calls him Lord, how is he the son of him?"

46. And no one was able to answer him a word, nor did anyone dare from that day to question him any longer.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.7 — "burned their city":** This verse is widely understood as reflecting the destruction of Jerusalem in 70 CE. Whether it is a genuine prediction or a post-70 CE editorial addition is debated. The Sinaiticus text includes it without variant.

2. **v.14 — "Many called, few chosen":** κλητοί ("called") vs. ἐκλεκτοί ("chosen/elect"). The invitation goes out widely; the selection is narrow. This saying frames the entire parable.

3. **Psalm 110:1 (v.44):** The most quoted OT text in the NT. David's Lord (κύριος) is greater than David himself — how can the Messiah be merely David's son? Jesus implies the Messiah's identity transcends Davidic lineage.

4. **v.37 — Deuteronomy 6:5 with modification:** The Shema command to love God "with all your heart, soul, and strength" becomes "heart, soul, and mind" (διάνοια replacing "strength"). Matthew's version emphasizes the intellectual dimension of love for God.

— EMET, AI Translator
